title: Proof Boundary — Clarity as an Economic Instrument
supported_claims: Role ambiguity is negatively associated with job performance across meta-analytic research.
Role ambiguity is a plausible source of organizational friction and economic cost.
Clarity can reduce avoidable translation and escalation work when embedded into operating systems.
The current draft presents clarity as an architectural leadership capability rather than a communication style.
unsupported_claims: Any specific percentage reduction in cost, delay, rework, or decision time.
Any universal causal claim that clarity alone produces economic gains.
Any quantified return-on-investment claim not supported by verified intervention data.
